revision.comment: Bot: mark 2 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24167314344|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:鎮国寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Chinkokuji Temple 20150502.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|Omuro school|qid=Q420739|1=Shingon Omuro School}}|prefecture={{ill|Genkai|qid=Q11572305|1=Genkai}}; {{ill|Munakata|qid=Q819603|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.834583333333335|N|130.5176111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}|deity={{ill|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|1=Vairocana}}|part_of={{ill|33 pilgrimage sites of Kyūshū-Saigoku|qid=Q11370337|1=33 pilgrimage sites of Kyūshū-Saigoku}}}} {{otheruses|Temple in Fukuoka Prefecture|Temple in Shanxi Province|Chinkoku-ji (Shanxi Province)|qq=}} {{infobox religious building |name = Chinkoku-ji |native_name = 鎮国寺 |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = [[File:Chinkokuji Temple 20150502.JPG|240px]]
境内遠景 |address = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|qid=Q819603|lt=Munakata City|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}} 吉田966 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 50 | 緯度秒 = 04.5 | 経度度 = 130 | 経度分 = 31 | 経度秒 = 03.4 |山号 = Byōbu-yama |宗派 = {{ill|Shingon Munmuro-ha|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Munmuro-ha|1=Shingon Omuro School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q420739}} |寺格 = {{ill|Special Head Temple|ja|別格本山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Head Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本山|1=honzan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24887668}}
{{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|qid=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}}の{{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}}・{{ill|Bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |本尊 = {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}} |創建年 = （伝）{{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daido Gen|1=Daidō}}（{{ill|806|en|806|qid=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}}） |開山 = （伝）{{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kobo Daishi|1=Kūkai}} |正式名 = Byōbu-yama Chinkoku-ji |別称 = |札所等 = {{ill|Kyushu Saigoku Sacred Sites|ja|九州西国霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Saigoku Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州西国三十三箇所|1=33 pilgrimage sites of Kyūshū-Saigoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370337}} No. 31
{{ill|Kyushu 88 Sacred Sites|ja|九州八十八箇所霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu 88 Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550945}} No. 88
{{ill|Kyushu 36 Fudō Sacred Sites|ja|九州三十六不動霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu 36 Fudō Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550946}} No. 34
{{ill|Kyushu 33 Kannon Sacred Sites|ja|九州三十三観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu 33 Kannon Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135550947|1=Kyushu 33 Kannon Sacred Sites}} No. 1 |文化財 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|label=Chinkoku-ji (鎮国寺)|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|float=center|relief=1|width=256|qq=}} }} '''Chinkoku-ji''' (鎮国寺) is a temple located in Yoshida, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|qid=Q819603|lt=Munakata City|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. Its sect is {{ill|Shingon Munmuro-ha|ja|真言宗御室派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Munmuro-ha|1=Shingon Omuro School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q420739}}, and it is recognized as a Special Head Temple. The mountain name is Byōbu-yama, and its principal image is {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}. It is designated as the 31st sacred site of the {{ill|Kyushu Saigoku Sacred Sites|ja|九州西国霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Saigoku Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州西国三十三箇所|1=33 pilgrimage sites of Kyūshū-Saigoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370337}}, the 88th of the {{ill|Kyushu 88 Sacred Sites|ja|九州八十八箇所霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu 88 Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550945}}, the 34th of the {{ill|Kyushu 36 Fudō Sacred Sites|ja|九州三十六不動霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu 36 Fudō Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550946}}, and the 1st of the {{ill|Kyushu 33 Kannon Sacred Sites|ja|九州三十三観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu 33 Kannon Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135550947|1=Kyushu 33 Kannon Sacred Sites}}. In former times, it served as the {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}} of {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|qid=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}}. == Overview == According to temple legend, when {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kobo Daishi|1=Kūkai}} set out on the 16th mission to Tang China aboard a Japanese envoy ship, he encountered a violent storm at sea. In his prayers to the guardian deities of the ocean – including the {{ill|Munakata Three Gods|ja|宗像三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Gods|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10948069}} – a manifestation of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} appeared among the waves and, wielding a sharp sword in his right hand, dispersed the surging waters to calm the storm, allowing him to safely reach Tang. Subsequently, in Chang'an (present-day {{ill|Xi'an|ja|西安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xi'an|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西安市|1=Xi'an|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5826}}), he received the secret esoteric teachings of Shingon from the priest Keika of {{ill|Qinglong Temple (Xi'an)|en|Qinglong Temple (Xi'an)|qid=Q10565990|lt=Qinglong Temple|1=Qinglong Temple (Xi'an)}}. In the year {{ill|806|en|806|qid=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}}, after arriving at Hakata and returning to Japan, he first paid his respects at {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|qid=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}}. At that time, upon witnessing auspicious clouds gathering over Byōbu-yama, he commenced his ritual practices within a rock cave in the inner sanctuary. There he received an oracle proclaiming, "This land is destined to be the principal sacred site for protecting the nation." Consequently, he established a temple and named it '''Byōbu-yama Chinkoku-ji'''. Around this period, Kūkai remained in Dazaifu for several years. It is the private temple of {{ill|Tatebe Yūdō|ja|立部祐道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe Yūdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 50th head priest of Munmuro-ha at Ninna-ji, and former chief of the sect. Additionally, his father, Tatebe Zuiyū, served as the 43rd head priest of Munmuro-ha. == Temple Grounds == [[File:Purunus mume at Chinkokuji-Temple 01.JPG|thumb|Ume blossoms blooming in front of the main hall, February 2014]] Situated on a small hill immediately east of {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|qid=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}}, across the {{ill|Tsurugawa River|ja|釣川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugawa River|1=Tsuri River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11648228}}, the temple stands in a serene location. The grounds are planted with a diverse array of flowering trees, earning it the nickname “Temple of Flowers.” The main hall was rebuilt in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|qid=Q1146135|lt=Keian era|1=Keian}} (1650) under the patronage of {{ill|Kuroda Tadanobu|ja|黒田忠之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Tadanobu|1=Tadayuki Kuroda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11678600}}, the second feudal lord of the Fukuoka Domain, and has been designated as a cultural property by Munakata City. The wooden standing statue of {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|qid=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, a secret Buddha displayed only on April 28, is recognized as a National Important Cultural Property, and the group of statues in the Five Buddhas Hall is designated as a cultural property by Fukuoka Prefecture. == Souvenirs == The specialty of Munakata City is the “Chinkoku” manju—a confection closely associated with Chinkoku-ji. It is produced by the confectionery shop Chinkoku Manju no Tsuzuka. == Access == * From {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|JR九州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州旅客鉄道|1=Kyushu Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q498366}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|qid=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}, board a {{ill|Nishitetsu Bus|ja|西鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Bus|1=Nishitetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629843}} bound for Kaminato Wharf (神湊波止場) and alight at “Munakata Taisha-mae”; then walk for 17 minutes (1.4 km). * By car, the temple is located 17 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|qid=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}} at the {{ill|Wakamiya Interchange|ja|若宮インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Interchange|1=Wakamiya interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1575782}}, or 16 km from the {{ill|Koga Interchange|ja|古賀インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koga Interchange|1=Koga interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1577981}}. * Parking is available. == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|qid=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}} * {{ill|Kyushu Saigoku Sacred Sites|ja|九州西国霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Saigoku Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九州西国三十三箇所|1=33 pilgrimage sites of Kyūshū-Saigoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370337}} == External Links == * [https://www.chinkokuji.or.jp/ Shingon Munmuro-ha Special Head Temple Byōbu-yama Chinkoku-ji] {{translated page|ja|鎮国寺|version=103216624|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鎮国寺]] {{Commons category|Chinkoku-ji}} {{wikidata link|Q11651157}} [[Category:九州地方にある日本庭園]] [[Category:九州西国三十三箇所]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:宗像市の建築物]] [[Category:宗像市の歴史]] [[Category:Munakata shrines]] [[Category:Temples of Shingon-shū Omuro-ha]] [[Category:Jingū-ji]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:福岡県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Kūkai]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
